background artscience museum is the cultural institution in singapore that explores the intersection between art, science, technology and culture. it is the cultural component of marina bay sands. since opening in february 2011, the museum has staged large-scale exhibitions by some of the world’s most famous artists, including leonardo da vinci; salvador dalí; andy warhol; vincent van gogh and m.c. escher. the museum has also commissioned new work as well as presenting significant exhibitions that explore aspects of science such as big data, particle physics, paleontology, marine biology and space exploration. the museum presents exhibitions and programmes about art and science using a combination of intellectual discussion, education, innovative design and interactive content. featuring 21 gallery spaces totaling nearly 5000m2, the architect-designed lotus-inspired building has played host to major international touring exhibitions from renowned museums and galleries around the world, including the british museum and the science museum in london; the american museum of natural history in new york; centre de cultura contemporània, barcelona; the ambrosiana, milan; the mori art museum, japan and many others. artscience museum also originates and produces exhibitions in-house and runs a full programme of education, events, activities and learning opportunities for a wide range of audiences. the exhibitions department at artscience museum is responsible for developing and implementing the programme of temporary and permanent exhibitions that reaches over 500,000 visitors annually. please refer to our website for more details about our exhibitions and our work. job scope the registrar/senior registrar at artscience museum will be responsible for effective opportunity to join the kids cancer centre as a drug access navigator, as part of a leading precision medicine program. what you'll be doing the kids cancer centre (kcc) located at the sydney children with high-risk or relapsed cancer. the kcc is currently seeking to appoint a paediatric oncology drug access navigator to manage the pharmaceutical care of patients enrolled on zero at a national level. this includes patients accessing: • standard treatments • novel drug treatments and combinations that have been recommended following comprehensive molecular profiling. drug access for paediatric patients in australia is an ongoing challenge, particularly with the recommendation of many new, targeted agents coming from the comprehensive molecular analysis undertaken in the zero the head of engineering will oversee all engineering activities for the upstream palm oil business, including managing pks production, civil, and mep projects. key responsibilities include developing strategies, managing budgets, ensuring compliance with iso, rspo, and ispo standards, and optimizing operations. the role requires at least 10 years of experience in palm oil mill operations and strong leadership in engineering project management. client details the client is a prominent player in the palm oil plantation sector, managing both upstream and downstream operations. their upstream business focuses on plantation management, palm oil production, and processing through palm kernel crushing stations (pks). meanwhile, the downstream side handles refining, distribution, and other value-added activities. they are committed to sustainable practices, adhering to international standards like iso, rspo, and ispo to ensure environmental and social responsibility. description oversee the development and execution of the engineering work plan and budget (rka) to ensure timely completion, efficient resource allocation, and adherence to company guidelines. develop strategies and implement production plans for palm kernel crushing stations (pks), ensuring targets for schedule, volume, and quality are met. oversee all civil and mep engineering tasks to ensure they comply with standards. strategize and manage civil and mep projects, ensuring timely and cost-efficient completion in line with contracts and specifications. evaluate budget plans, equipment, and material usage for ongoing and future projects. ensure all engineering processes adhere to iso, rspo, and ispo standards. drive continuous improvements by optimizing methods, proposing new technologies, and revising sops for efficiency.implement health, safety, and environmental programs to minimize work-related risks and manage waste in compliance with regulations. lead, develop, and mentor the engineering team. set individual kpis, foster a culture aligned with company values, and ensure continuous development through training and knowledge sharing. review and sign off on all operational documents and reports, ensuring accuracy and preventing errors or fraud. establish quality objectives and monitor performance metrics across engineering operations. profile at least 10 years in the palm oil industry, with a minimum of 3 years as chief engineering/head of engineering or 7 years as regional mill controller/gm processing proven experience managing civil and mechanical engineering projects in large-scale operations. a bachelor's degree in mechanical, civil, or electrical engineering. strong leadership abilities, capable of managing large teams and complex engineering projects across multiple disciplines (civil, mechanical, and mep). demonstrated ability to develop and implement strategies that optimize operations, ensuring cost-effectiveness and adherence to safety and environmental standards. deep understanding of pks (palm kernel crushing station) operations and related engineering processes. familiarity with iso, rspo, ispo, and other sustainability standards, with the ability to ensure compliance. a track record of driving continuous improvement initiatives and implementing innovative solutions to enhance operational efficiency. strong analytical and decision-making skills to handle complex engineering and operational challenges. excellent communication skills, able to provide clear direction to teams and collaborate with other departments and stakeholders. capable of fostering a collaborative, results-driven work culture aligned with the company's core values. a commitment to mentoring and developing the engineering team, ensuring skill enhancement and preparing internal candidates for leadership roles. job offer a market-aligned salary, complemented by a comprehensive benefits package that includes health insurance, retirement plans, and other perks. the chance to lead a critical engineering division in a well-established and expanding palm oil business, with significant influence over strategic decisions and operational outcomes. the opportunity for career advancement within the company's hierarchy, with potential pathways to higher executive roles in the future. to apply online please click the 'apply' button below. the earthquake commission (eqc), kōmihana rūwhenua, is a crown entity with a mission to reduce the impact on people and property when natural disasters occur. our country's natural hazard risk profile is complex, so we are focussed on supporting a healthy and prosperous new zealand through readiness for and recovery from natural disasters, risk financing, and building new zealand's resilience to the impacts of natural disasters. te whiwhinga mahi | the opportunity the purpose of this position is to design and deliver business intelligence (bi) and analytical content across eqc's platforms incorporating geospatial and other data from eqc and third parties. the role has a focus on working with geospatial data across bi and analytics platform architecture layers e.g. from data replication, to transformation, and visualization. ko wai koe | about you you'll be a qualified and experienced geospatial data engineer/data scientist, who has the ability to work well within public sector administrative processes and structures. you'll have a sound knowledge and understanding of geospatial data lifecycle, including data pipelines, analytics, and presentation techniques and methods, in an azure environment. you'll have strong understanding of gis data lifecycle best practices, including data profiling and data quality. you'll have experience working with geospatial data in both esri arcgis and sql server. you'll have experience in designing, developing, and testing geospatial pipelines, for example using postgresql and postgrest. you'll have the capabilities of producing/reviewing technical documentation, e.g. design documentation including data models and design decisions, 'as-built', user guides, testing plans. why eqc is a great place to work eqc recognises that our people are our greatest asset, and we prioritise their wellbeing. along with a competitive salary, five weeks annual leave, an employee wellbeing fund and ongoing professional development, we provide an inclusive, engaging and purpose-driven culture. our commitment to a diverse and inclusive workforce is because we know our culture thrives when we get this right, and that our customers get the best from us. we work to draw on the differences in who we are, what we've experienced, and how we think. we make a difference for all new zealanders and strive to increasingly reflect the diversity of communities we serve by including and valuing perspectives from all backgrounds including ethnicity, religion, gender, age, accessibility needs, sexual orientation, and national origin. how to apply for a more detailed understanding of the position or if you're ready to apply, please visit our careers website by clicking apply. if you have any questions about the role or require further information or assistance, please contact us at or 0800 326 243. applications close wednesday, 18 may 2022 at 5pm.

description jawatan : pembantu teknikal - teknologi maklumat gred: d4 (rm 1500 - rm2345) kumpulan: sokongan koperasi tenaga nasional berhad (koptnb) ingin mempelawa warganegara malaysia untuk memohon dan mengisi jawatan kosong seperti yang dinyatakan. tawaran dibuka kepada calon yang mempunyai kelayakan seperti berikut. syarat & kriteria kelayakan: 1. mempunyai kelayakan sekurang-kurangnya spm, stpm, skm tahap 2 atau diploma dalam bidang yang berkaitan. 2. berpengalaman kerja 1-2 tahun dalam bidang teknologi maklumat adalah satu kelebihan. 3. mempunyai pengetahuan, kemahiran dan kepakaran yang mendalam mengenai perkakasan, peralatan dan sistem komputer serta mengikuti perkembangan teknologi maklumat terkini. 4. bertanggungjawab, amanah, proaktif, berdedikasi dan komited serta memiliki kemahiran komunikasi yang baik. 5. lepasan graduan digalakkan untuk memohon. skop tugas:- bertanggungjawab dalam melaksanakan tugas-tugas merangkumi penyelenggaraan sistem komputer seperti penyelenggaraan pencegahan dan penyelenggaraan kelengkapan it serta tugas-tugas penyelenggaraan aplikasi perkomputeran dan operasi sistem dalam koperasi. seterusnya, menerima dan menyempurnakan tugas yang diberikan oleh pihak atasan dari semasa ke semasa faedah / kelebihan: - persekitaran kerja yang baik - latihan dan kursus berkaitan disediakan - feadah perubatan - kemudahan pengangkutan awam lokasi kerja : hq, koperasi tnb, jalan bangsar, kuala lumpur. company pada awal tahun 1951, mesyuarat pertama diadakan yang dipengerusikan oleh encik m. pakianathan, penolong akauntan lembaga letrik pusat telah bersetuju untuk menubuhkan sebuah koperasi khusus bagi kakitangan lembaga letrik pusat yang bergaji bulanan penubuhan koperasi ini telah dipersetujui oleh encik f.g. egerton, pengurus besar lembaga letrik negara pada masa tersebut. permohonan pendaftaran telah dibuat di pendaftar pertubuhan koperasi pada 1hb ogos 1951. maka rasmilah pendaftaran syarikat kerjasama jimat cermat dan pinjaman wang lembaga letrik pusat sendirian berhad dengan nombor pendaftaran 261/51-1449. pada jun 1965, koperasi telah menukar nama kepada syarikat kerjasama jimat cermat dan pinjaman wang lembaga letrik negara sendirian berhad, dimana perubahan ini adalah sejajar dengan pertukaran nama lembaga letrik pusat kepada lembaga letrik negara. objektif utama koperasi adalah menggalakkan aktiviti jimat cermat serta menjalankan fungsi-fungsi pinjaman, pengguna, perkhidmatan dan perumahan. memandangkan sebutan nama ini yang agak panjang, pada tahun 1967, koperasi telah menukar namanya kepada koperatif lembaga letrik berhad. pada september 1990, selaras dengan dasar penswastaan negara yang turut melibatkan lembaga letrik negara ditransformasikan dari sebuah lembaga kepada syarikat korporat bernama tenaga nasional berhad, koperasi juga telah mengambil langkah yang sewajarnya untuk menukar nama kepada koperasi tenaga nasional berhad sehingga kini.

job_title: head of nursing - tūmuaki mahi tāpuhi (postgraduate or undergraduate)
job_ad_details: wintec's te kura mō ngā mahi tiaki i te hapori (centre for health and social practice) is seeking a highly motivated head of nursing to join the centre's inter-professional management team, based in hamilton. if you value working collaboratively with other health professional educators in a dynamic and rapidly changing environment, delivering nursing education for local, regional and international benefit, this is an opportunity not to be missed! about the role in this role you will be responsible for managing the day-to-day academic activities of the nursing team (postgraduate or undergraduate), and ensuring all resources are in place for a quality learning environment. you will guide and support staff to achieve agreed teaching, research and industry engagement outcomes and to grow professionally. you will lead a team of nurse educators responsible for teaching bicultural programmes either undergraduate or postgraduate informed by an excellent understanding of treaty-based practice. maintaining productive working relationships within the nursing programmes and across wintec will also be important. similarly, maintaining current industry connections and fostering new ones is key. not only will you keep abreast of the latest information and technology, you will create valuable opportunities for students and manage the delivery and development of programmes for flexible delivery locally and internationally. you will be a strategic thinker who has a vision for the future of nursing. are you an ideal candidate? do you have relevant industry experience as well as a professional qualification(s) in nursing? with your highly developed communication skills, line management experience and the ability to build and maintain strong relationships with relevant industry sectors, you could find your next role in our centre's energetic and future focused management team. ideally applicants will have the following: passion and clear vision for the future of nursing strong nursing background in both educational and clinical contexts proven leadership competence within a complex, fast paced, changing environment demonstrated understanding of te tiriti o waitangi a passion for supporting students to succeed an understanding of the tertiary education sector if applicable, appointment with a further title of associate professor or professor will be considered, commensurate with relevant academic experience. working at wintec wintec continues to be one of the leading tertiary institutes by keeping up with industry trends and focusing on innovation. you'll be supported to stay industry connected and have generous opportunities to develop your career through the professional development opportunities available to you. working in hamilton hamilton is one of new zealand's fastest growing cities. it has an urban and semi-urban population of about 180,000, plenty of schools - public and private, a temperate climate and distinctive rural character. the city sits on the banks of the waikato river, linked by a series of bridges. the city is generally safe, quiet and without major traffic jams. house prices are more affordable than many of new zealand's major centres (such as auckland, wellington and christchurch). hamilton has its own domestic airport and is 90 minutes away from the country's main international airport in auckland. hamilton's central location - in the middle of the north island - makes it an ideal springboard for beaches, lakes and mountains. it's less than two hours' drive to the popular tourist destinations of rotorua, taupō and the coromandel peninsula. join the centre for health and social practice and take the next exciting step in your career. lead change, shape the world at wintec - apply now and accept a leadership challenge.

job_title: sea internal audit manager (sea risk advisory)
job_ad_details: what impact will you make? at deloitte, we offer a unique and exceptional career experience to inspire and empower talents like you to make an impact that matters for our clients, people and community. whatever your aspirations, deloitte offers you a highly inclusive, collaborative workplace and unrivalled opportunities to realize your full potential. we are always looking for people with the relentless energy to push themselves further, and to find new avenues and unique ways to reach our shared goals. so what are you waiting for? join the winning team now. work you'll do demonstrate understanding of business processes, internal control risk management, it controls, and related regulatory and compliance standards. perform internal audit assurance activities (internal audits over financial, operational, compliance, it, sox and qars), consult with engagement leadership and clients on strategic plans and other business matters, and help to anticipate emerging risks for our clients. use problem solving and critical thinking skills to quickly identify internal control deficiencies, evaluate their risk implications, and draw the appropriate conclusions to best advise our clients. apply a general knowledge of the technologies used to build complex it environments. facilitate use of technology-based tools or methodologies to review, design, and implement products and services. support client engagements by helping plan the audit approach and scope, preparing the audit program, determining auditing procedures, seeing the audit process through completion, and applying internal audit standards in accordance with the institute of internal auditors. actively participate in decision making with engagement management and seek to understand the broader impact of current decisions. build and nurture positive working relationships with clients, by providing high quality deliverables and communications. create internal control documentation for the engagement including narratives, process and data flows, and other supporting work papers. play a substantive role with project management by supporting engagement planning, economics, billing and staffing; providing regular status reports for the client, while supervising junior staff. exercise professional skepticism, judgment and adhere to the code of ethics while on engagements. your role as a leader at deloitte, we believe in the importance of empowering our people to be leaders at all levels. we expect our people to embrace and live our purpose and shared values, challenging themselves everyday to identify issues that are most important to our clients, our people and the communities, and to make an impact that matters. in addition to living our purpose, managers across our firm are expected to: understand the goals of our clients and deloitte, align our people to these objectives by setting clear priorities and direction. influence clients, teams, and individuals positively. lead by example and provide equal opportunity for people to grow, develop and succeed. establish positive relationship with senior stakeholders. delivers exceptional client service. maximizes results and drives high performance from people while fostering collaboration across businesses and geographies. manage diverse teams within a highly inclusive team culture where people are respected and valued for their contribution. develop high-performing teams through challenging and meaningful opportunities and recognize them for the impact that they make. requirements must have 8-10 years of internal audit experience. must have bachelor's degree (in accounting, business administration, computer science, information systems, management information systems, accounting information systems or related field). having professional certifications icaew, acca, cima, cia, cisa or related field are preferred. due to volume of applications, we regret only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

important disclaimer customer service is merely one interaction along the whole customer journey with any given company. it’s the help a customer needs and is a reactive approach. we're changing this. we are looking for two more candidates who will work collaboratively together and will get real joy out of watching one another grow. you will be delivering what clients didn’t know they needed and take a proactive approach. you will be someone who makes every customer interaction memorable and also encompass all touchpoints and champion the entire customer journey. do you think you are someone who can help us deliver on one of our brand promises - every client matters - and shape how customers perceive watchful & crosbies security? about us we're a true family-owned and operated security business based in pukekohe. we've been operating for 5 years and have been experiencing substantial growth across all our service offerings, and we are keen to continue the momentum. many people haven't worked in a small business growing that fast. one day you'll arrive at work, and three processes have changed overnight. that's how fast we change to keep up with 80-100% yoy growth. you need to be prepared. about watchful. watchful. is a subsidiary of crosbies security limited. watchful makes clients question why post-incident cctv investigations were ever a thing. come and join us for an interview to see what this brand new and exciting service offering is all about! so, what are we doing day in and day out? we assemble and build our security technology and offer market-leading solutions to our clients. that's what chatgpt wrote! really, we stop bad guys from slowing down building sites across auckland, and we do the best job of anyone in our market by a long stretch. we continually work hard to achieve the best security outcomes for our clients. that's also what chatgpt wrote. but what it didn't write were our values. those were written by our team. they are: • determined - don't show up unless you're going to actually give a damn to solve the tough challenges you'll face. • committed to whanaungatanga - care about your clients (they pay your wages, really), and of course, your peers. • lateral thinker - in-the-box solutions will kill our business so if this is you, please, scroll on! • positive team player - help out your buds to see them succeed and do it silently when no one is watching. • ground-breaker - think of ideas to shape the future of the business. • high standards - if you know what we mean when we say, “dress one standard higher than the audience you're addressing,” then you'll have no trouble with this one. about the role we can't teach attitude or cultural fit. so your interview will be to ensure you have a great attitude, and you'll fit within our culture. bring drive, ambition, new ideas, and hunger to our team, and you'll be well on your way to delivering on our values. so what will you do day in, day out? as part of our customer service monitoring team, you will be literally stopping bad guys (kind of like how they do it in the movies) by doing the following: you will need to make quick, methodical decisions like how neo had to choose between the red and the blue pill in the matrix – monitoring over 5000 camera activations a month and coordinating with your fellow customer experience officers to deliver on our values and brand promises. you will need to have an eye for detail and think of yourself as sherlock holmes – liaising with the police and other emergency services to provide evidence, information, and real-time updates on what and where the bad guys are so you can help the good guys. you will possess the ability to be an excellent communicator and with that great power, comes great responsibilities – coordinate with internal stakeholders (sales and service team) to make sure we are kept up-to-date on what is happening to our overnight operations. you will play an integral part in shaping the improvement of our customer experience, everyday. what does "good" look like? kpis are a thing in our business, but for good reason: they set you and us up for a successful working relationship. you will be measured on the following key kpis: • response times to camera activations • job open-to-closure rates • number of tickets logged • customer nps: apple's nps is 72. ours is 67. we don't have far to go! so yeah, this is a critical measure for your customers. benefits and perks: • we are offering a base of $24 - $26 per hour for the right candidate, with a review after six months of being employed with crosbies security. • shift work with the opportunity to pick up more shifts. • free life insurance benefit ($300k or 4x salary, whichever is lower) after three months of employment. • free private healthcare insurance benefit ($1,000 excess) including pre-existing conditions covered, through nib, after three months of employment. • leadership development program available for those who wish to progress their career. modules and presentations have to date included “how to talk to anyone”, “having tough conversations”, “atomic habits”, “a-players” and more. skills and experience: we are looking for someone who has the following skills and experience: • the right to work in new zealand. • excellent english communication, both written and verbal. non-negotiable. like seriously, non-negotiable. • restricted or full nz driver's license. • confidence in their telecommunication skills (ideal but not required). crosbies brand promises: finally, our offering is successful because of three things that you'll help maintain and grow: we keep site teams moving, every day. every client matters. provide solutions that are the most trusted in the market. as part of our interview process, you'll tell us what you think they mean for this role. so be prepared. how to apply: if this sounds like you, then please apply now. include your cv, plus a short covering letter stating why you think you will make an excellent customer service representative. candidate timeline: • we are moving quickly. after application, if you are shortlisted, you will be contacted within 72 hours. interviews will take place very quickly, as we are working on a deadline of appointing all candidates within 30 days or less. that said, we are cautious and selective with who we bring into our business and our due diligence will not be reduced due to our faster hiring timeline.

1.1 engagement/project manager one of the core competencies for a manila based em is to be able to run with the project once the requirements are documented and complete it and deliver to download connect. this means: the person should be able to draw up a project plan should be able to understand and work with jira work with all stakeholders to complete the project needs experience with software sdlc should be proactive, follow up and drive the project the person will work collaboratively with the em onsite to mitigate risk and track the project. we do expect this person to be client facing so those skills are important as well. this is not a comprehensive list for all skills but the core ones that we will focus on while evaluating. (a) required qualifications: (i) four (4) year degree in business administration, health informatics, computer science or related field or equivalent experience; (ii) three (3) to five (5) years’ experience in it project management with a minimum of two (2) years in software implementation project manager, preferably in a payor (health plan) environment with familiarity of claims processing and claims payment operations; (iii) experience in healthcare claims it project management; (iv) experience in software implementation project management including conflict resolution, resource allocation and prioritization; (v) demonstrated experience managing highly visible strategic and demanding customers; (vi) demonstrated ability to manage multiple concurrent projects with significant visibility to the client executive teams; (vii) strong foundation in project management principles, processes and methodology; (viii) strong influential management experience; (ix) must have strong and effective communication and presentation skills for effective communication and presentation skills for external client facing as well as internal communications; and (x) project management professional (pmp) certification strongly preferred. (b) primary responsibilities: (i) lead the implementation and support of multiple, large, complex projects by working with a diverse, virtual, cross-functional team, including working closely with account management to manage the chc customer and to define the chc customer roadmap on an ongoing basis and leading the service delivery team to execute on the chc customer roadmap. this constitutes forty percent (40%) of the engagement/project manager’s responsibilities. (ii) manage the progress of the projects ensuring an on schedule, on budget delivery, effectively manage change and ensure that the project team is tracking to chc contractual commitments. this constitutes twenty percent (20%) of the engagement/project manager’s responsibilities. (iii) coordinate and maintain interdepartmental relationships/dependencies associated with major software implementation projects, which includes the following and constitutes twenty percent (20%) of the engagement/project manager’s responsibilities: (a) strong foundation in project management principles; (b) detailed and high-level knowledge of ms office suite of products; (c) meeting facilitation and leadership; (d) project management coaching and mentoring; (e) issue and risk management; (f) project metric and measurement; (g) flexible time management and priority setting; and (h) resource management. (iv) utilize project management tools to facilitate planning and tracking of large, complex projects and communicating effectively with all stakeholders for regular status reporting and on escalations. this constitutes ten percent (10%) of the engagement/project manager’s responsibilities. (v) build team environment, assuring effective project communication, managing expectations with all levels, effectively managing conflicts and maintaining strong working relationships with team members and stakeholders at all levels. this constitutes ten percent (10%) of the engagement/project manager’s responsibilities.

earning potential (starting hourly rate is $26.24 - plus overtime/penal rates for weekend and night shifts) structured pathway for career progression the opportunity we have: this is an exciting opportunity for passionate, reliable, and trustworthy individuals to secure a permanent position with an iconic kiwi company. our safety protectors are trained staff who make sure that employees, contractors, and the public don't get hurt by trains. during training you will learn how to take ownership of the following duties: communicating with staff and contractors working on the rail assist with the preparation of safety plans review land entry permits for contractors liaising customers, subcontractors, and train control daily conducting a job plan and safety briefing for a worksite complete forms and documents as and when required you will need to understand and follow the rules and processes to keep people safe. what skills are we looking for: you must be able to follow rules 100% of the time you will be a confident communicator, able to interact with people either face-to-face or over the radio you will be passionate about keeping people and keeping them safe at work you will enjoy working outdoors as part of a team, or sometimes independently it is critical you are alert, conscious of your surroundings, and aware of where people in the rail corridor are at all times. being able to adapt to changing conditions and being able to think on your feet will make you an ideal candidate for this job. it is also very important to build rapport with key stakeholders such as project managers, project engineers, track workers, train control etc it is essential you have: full driver's license essential excellent communication skills (both verbal and written) and radio shifts full paid training and support to gain rail qualifications, certificates and required licenses to set you up for success structured career development prospects within the rail industry enjoy staff benefits such as access to discounted travel, accommodation, and insurance you must also be able to pass a ministry of justice check and complete a mandatory medical and drug test to be job_title: process operators - annies fruit bars - morning and afternoon shifts
job_ad_details: our business since the time of the hekenga, our people have displayed the qualities of innovation, perseverance, flexibility and adaptability. our tupuna were creative, astute and forward thinking people who gifted us our land, whakapapa and cultural identity. kono nz lp is a business that thrives off the taonga or gifts that have been handed down to us by our tupuna and include land, sea and natural resources. kono is a top 100, premium new zealand food and beverage company with a unique 500-year business plan, te pae tawhiti. our interests span wine, horticulture, fruit bars and seafood and we export to over 25 countries throughout the world. our products encapsulate the pure taste of aotearoa new zealand and are produced sustainably, akin to the maori values of our owners. our business is diverse, from farming and harvesting, to processing and the marketing of seafood, wine, cider, apples, pears, kiwifruit, hops and natural fruit bars. the role as a production/process operator, you play an active role in ensuring that the fruit bars are continually delivered at a high standard to all our customers in an effective, quality focused and productive manner. we have a number of permanent roles available at our annies fruit bar factory in grovetown, on both the morning and afternoon shifts. the roles are varied, and we need people who have a good level of fitness as some heavy lifting is required. great time management is also essential. in return we offer a great team environment along with a strong values-based outlook. this is a great opportunity to join a fantastic team. if you think you have got what it takes, then we want to hear from you. applications for this role close friday the 2nd of april 2021. to apply for this job go to: kononz.recruitmenthub.co.nz/vacancies and enter ref code: 5406767. applications close: 02 april 2021
